notes: Did play the game just for a few minutes, coming to the conclusion that I don't like it. It was working without any issues for that time.
title: Backbone: Prologue

notes: It works out of the box, but the frame rate is not great, I get an approx average of 20fps with lots of stutters. I get double the FPS on Hitman 2 which is a heavy title, game is probably not well optimised or has issues with proton.
title: Backbone: Prologue

notes: Framerate is not very good, less than 40 fps, other than that everything is fine.
title: Backbone: Prologue

notes:
The game worked, but at the first launch it gave out only 9 fps. On the next launches, there were 20 sometimes 30 fps, which is 15-20 less than on Windows. I was unable to either Linux or Windows to go beyond the office, no one knocked on the door.
And recently, the game itself disappeared from my disk to Linux, and no longer wants to be installed. Proton writes that the exe file was not found. Trying to check the integrity of the cache and reinstalling the game did not help.
title: Backbone: Prologue

notes: The game ran flawlessly except for two minor things. The first one is that the game is not native to Linux but steam does not automatically make it use proton. Had to go to properties to make it use latest proton. The second is that the ending video that is suppose to play when you've finished the game only plays audio. The video itself was black. FPS performance was not 60 fps but it was most definitely playable at 35-50+ FPS.
title: Backbone: Prologue
